core_mechanism: A parameter-shared deep composition of nonlinear maps that aggregates many fine-grained (high-rate) inputs onto a coarse (low-rate) target by stacking per-stage nonlinear transformations with weights tied across stages, so the approximator's depth can grow without the parameter count scaling with the number of inputs, with a universal-approximation guarantee and a non-asymptotic error bound.
brief_reason: The abstract replaces linear frequency-alignment transformations with a deep neural network per alignment ('depth-separable network') plus a parameter-sharing mechanism across stages that enables a deeper network for large sets of higher-frequency predictors, and establishes an approximation theory with a non-asymptotic prediction-error bound.
